messages.content:
Create a retrieval-oriented summary for this parent section of a document.
Use the child summaries below. Mention the topics, entities, dates, tables,
figures, definitions, procedures, claims, metrics, and evidence types covered
when they are visible. Do not invent facts. Return only the summary text.

Parent:
{
  "node_id": "0156",
  "title": "93 独家视角",
  "page_index": 94,
  "page_range": "94"
}

Child summaries:
[
  {
    "node_id": "0157",
    "title": "Youdao Ads、OneSight、ToBid、Joyce 游戏运营",
    "page_range": "94-95",
    "summary": "This section serves as a heading or index for content related to game operations (游戏运营). It explicitly lists four entities/tools: Youdao Ads, OneSight, ToBid, and Joyce. It can answer questions regarding which specific platforms or systems are categorized under game operations in this document. The provided text contains no dates, definitions, procedures, claims, tables, figures, metrics, or evidence types."
  },
  {
    "node_id": "0158",
    "title": "Youdao Ads",
    "page_range": "95",
    "summary": "This section (Page 95) outlines the strategic evolution of Youdao Ads and broader digital marketing trends driven by AI and data optimization. It addresses the following retrieval points:\n- **Topics:** AI-driven content marketing, advertising data optimization for high-value user acquisition, intelligent KOL/influencer marketing workflows, and the AIGC revolution in creative material production and full-chain marketing platforms.\n- **Entities:** AI/AIGC, Youdao Ads, target markets, hit/blockbuster games, KOLs (Key Opinion Leaders), large language models (LLMs), and brand GEO.\n- **Dates:** 2026 (projected milestone for AI collaboration and full-chain platform vision).\n- **Tables/Figures:** None mentioned across the subsections.\n- **Definitions:** No explicit definitions provided; contextually frames a \"full-chain AI intelligent marketing platform\" as an integrated system spanning the entire marketing lifecycle.\n- **Procedures:** AI analysis of target markets and successful game marketing logic to decode user preferences; real-time iteration of ad strategies for targeted acquisition; an end-to-end KOL campaign workflow (Insight → Screening → Connection → Review); and Youdao Ads' strategic upgrade from isolated AI functions to a unified operational workflow (content creation → material delivery → operation optimization → project management).\n- **Claims:** Content marketing precision and AI response speed are becoming primary competitive differentiators; data-driven iteration shifts competition away from traditional channel/budget reliance toward high LTV user capture; AI enables efficient, localized, and cost-effective KOL selection; and AIGC significantly boosts creative efficiency, generates culturally aligned viral content, and enhances long-term brand GEO through impr..."
  },
  {
    "node_id": "0163",
    "title": "OneSight",
    "page_range": "96",
    "summary": "This section (page 96) outlines strategic frameworks for game brand positioning and community engagement, focusing on narrative anchor differentiation and player co-creation paradigms.\n\n**Topics & Definitions:** Contrasts the \"character-centric path\" (treating in-game characters as primary interactive touchpoints for IP development) with the \"story-centric path\" (binding core cultural and narrative assets to drive audience engagement). Implicitly defines a \"player co-creation space\" as a social media-based channel designed for bidirectional communication and collaborative brand/product development.\n\n**Entities:** 《鸣潮》(Wuthering Waves) and 《原神》(Genshin Impact) exemplify the character-centric approach; 《燕云十六声》 represents the story-centric approach. Additional entities include social media platforms, core/skilled players, UGC creators, official content matrices, competitive tournament ecosystems, and game brands.\n\n**Procedures:** Character-centric operations involve organizing UGC campaigns synchronized with version updates and character anniversaries. Story-centric operations utilize overseas social media to analyze and disseminate historical/cultural backgrounds. Co-creation procedures establish bidirectional social media channels for feedback collection, suggestion implementation reporting, and core player workflow integration; they also link community ecosystems with tournaments via gameplay creation contests, UGC curation for official channels, and recruiting skilled players for secondary content dissemination.\n\n**Claims:** The character-centric model claims to successfully deepen long-term emotional attachment between users and the IP. The story-centric model claims to transition users from passive players into active co-creators of the game's worldview. Transparent..."
  },
  {
    "node_id": "0170",
    "title": "ToBid",
    "page_range": "97",
    "summary": "This section details the strategic evolution of the mobile ad monetization industry, targeting developers, advertisers, and application/user/ad placement value ecosystems. It outlines a two-phase efficiency reconstruction cycle: 2025 focuses on a transition to rationality and an “efficiency-first” model, while 2026 targets “structural dividends” through the optimization of existing traffic. Key conceptual shifts include moving from “scale-first” to “efficiency-first” paradigms and evolving from single-point eCPM optimization to holistic overall revenue optimization. Core metrics integrated into modern decision frameworks include eCPM, fill rate, frequency capping, and long-term ARPDAU. Recommended procedures involve replacing basic price sorting with global revenue judgment, unifying performance and user experience metrics into a single decision framework, and combining systematic platform capabilities with deep vertical-specific business insights. The section claims that slowing traffic growth and tighter advertiser budgets make short-term eCPM chasing unsustainable, asserting that future growth will stem from optimizing existing traffic rather than volume expansion, while generic monetization solutions are losing effectiveness. These points are supported by industry trend analysis, strategic forecasting, and conceptual framework comparisons; no tables or figures are referenced in this section."
  }
]

message.content: This parent section ("93 独家视角", pages 94–97) provides an exclusive perspective on modern game operations (游戏运营), organized around four core platforms: Youdao Ads, OneSight, ToBid, and Joyce. It covers AI-driven digital marketing, AIGC creative production, intelligent KOL workflows, game brand positioning, community co-creation, and mobile ad monetization strategy. Key entities include Youdao Ads, OneSight, ToBid, Joyce, AI/AIGC, LLMs, KOLs, 《鸣潮》(Wuthering Waves), 《原神》(Genshin Impact), 《燕云十六声》, UGC creators, developers, and advertisers. Relevant dates include 2025 (industry transition to an "efficiency-first" monetization model) and 2026 (projected milestone for full-chain AI marketing platforms and structural traffic optimization). The text defines a "full-chain AI intelligent marketing platform," a "player co-creation space," and contrasts "character-centric" versus "story-centric" IP development paths. Documented procedures include AI-driven market analysis and real-time ad iteration, end-to-end KOL campaign workflows (Insight → Screening → Connection → Review), version-synchronized UGC campaigns, bidirectional community feedback channels, and global revenue optimization frameworks that replace basic price sorting. Core claims assert that AI precision and data-driven iteration are primary competitive differentiators, character-centric models deepen long-term IP emotional attachment, story-centric approaches transform players into active co-creators, and short-term eCPM chasing is unsustainable amid slowing traffic growth and tighter budgets. Tracked metrics include eCPM, fill rate, frequency capping, long-term ARPDAU, and LTV. Evidence is supported by industry trend analysis, strategic forecasting, conceptual framework comparisons, and specific game case studies. No tables or figures are referenced in this section.

response_text: This parent section ("93 独家视角", pages 94–97) provides an exclusive perspective on modern game operations (游戏运营), organized around four core platforms: Youdao Ads, OneSight, ToBid, and Joyce. It covers AI-driven digital marketing, AIGC creative production, intelligent KOL workflows, game brand positioning, community co-creation, and mobile ad monetization strategy. Key entities include Youdao Ads, OneSight, ToBid, Joyce, AI/AIGC, LLMs, KOLs, 《鸣潮》(Wuthering Waves), 《原神》(Genshin Impact), 《燕云十六声》, UGC creators, developers, and advertisers. Relevant dates include 2025 (industry transition to an "efficiency-first" monetization model) and 2026 (projected milestone for full-chain AI marketing platforms and structural traffic optimization). The text defines a "full-chain AI intelligent marketing platform," a "player co-creation space," and contrasts "character-centric" versus "story-centric" IP development paths. Documented procedures include AI-driven market analysis and real-time ad iteration, end-to-end KOL campaign workflows (Insight → Screening → Connection → Review), version-synchronized UGC campaigns, bidirectional community feedback channels, and global revenue optimization frameworks that replace basic price sorting. Core claims assert that AI precision and data-driven iteration are primary competitive differentiators, character-centric models deepen long-term IP emotional attachment, story-centric approaches transform players into active co-creators, and short-term eCPM chasing is unsustainable amid slowing traffic growth and tighter budgets. Tracked metrics include eCPM, fill rate, frequency capping, long-term ARPDAU, and LTV. Evidence is supported by industry trend analysis, strategic forecasting, conceptual framework comparisons, and specific game case studies. No tables or figures are referenced in this section.
